description: Nepali New Year marks the start of 2082 BS and is celebrated across Nepal.
Celebrates birth, enlightenment, and nirvana of Lord Buddha (shared with Chandi Purnima).
Anniversary of Nepal becoming a republic.
Major Hindu festival observed by women for marital bliss and well-being.
Festival celebrating Radha and Lord Krishna.
Major festival in Kathmandu with masked dances and chariot processions.
Newar festival honoring departed souls and community celebration.
Celebrates the birth of Lord Krishna.
Celebrates the adoption of Nepal's constitution.
First day of Dashain; sacred jamara planted.
Sacred flowers and leaves brought home.
Eighth day of Dashain; Goddess worship.
Ninth day of Dashain; worship and rituals continue.
Main Dashain day; tika and jamara ceremony.
First day of Tihar; worshiping crows.
Worshiping dogs for loyalty and protection.
Cows honored; Goddess Laxmi worshiped for prosperity.
Worship of oxen or self depending on community.
Sisters bless brothers for long life and prosperity.
Sun worship festival observed by Mithila communities.
Full moon fasting and rituals; also Guru Nanak Jayanti for Sikh community.
Celebrates the sun's transition into Capricorn; marks the end of winter.
the new year of the Tamang community with traditional rituals and festivities.
Honors Saraswati, the goddess of knowledge, music, and arts.
Commemorates the martyrs who sacrificed their lives for the nation.
Fasting and night vigil dedicated to Lord Shiva.
Tamang New Year celebration with traditional rituals and festivities.
Celebrates the establishment of democracy in Nepal.
Holi celebration specific to the Himalayan region with unique customs and traditions.
Holi celebration specific to the Terai region with unique customs and traditions.
Celebration of women's achievements and promotion of gender equality.
Celebration honoring Falgunanda.
Traditional horse festival celebrated in Kathmandu.
Islamic festival marking the end of Ramadan, the holy month of fasting.
Celebration of Chaitra Dashain, a significant festival in Nepal.
Celebrates the birth of Lord Rama.
Nepali New Year marks the start of 2083 BS and is celebrated nationwide.
Auspicious day for new beginnings and charity.
Commemorates establishment of democracy.
Hindu fasting day dedicated to Lord Vishnu.
Celebrates birth, enlightenment and death of Lord Buddha.
Observed by the Kirat community as a cultural day.
Hindu fasting day dedicated to Lord Vishnu.
Celebrate democracy and the establishment of the republic in Nepal.
Traditional Newari festival where people eat a mixture of curd and beaten rice to celebrate the bond between brothers and sisters.
Celebrate by cooking and eating kheer (rice pudding) to honor the goddess Annapurna.
Worship of serpent deities.
Sacred thread ceremony for men and brother-sister bond celebration.
A Hindu festival celebrating the birth of Lord Krishna.
A traditional Newari festival where people visit their paternal grandparents' house and seek blessings.
A Hindu festival celebrated by women with fasting and prayers for marital bliss and the well-being of their spouses.
A Hindu festival celebrating the birth of Lord Ganesha, the god of wisdom and prosperity.
A Hindu festival dedicated to the sage Rishi, observed by women with fasting and rituals for purification.
A Hindu festival dedicated to Lord Vishwakarma, the divine architect and engineer.
A day to celebrate the adoption of the Constitution of Nepal.
A traditional Newar festival celebrated with chariot processions and various cultural events.
First day of Dashain, installing the Kalash for Goddess Durga.
Seventh day of Dashain, bringing sacred flowers and leaves to homes.
Eighth day of Dashain, Goddess Durga worshiped with animal sacrifices in some traditions.
Ninth day of Dashain, worshiping Goddess Durga and seeking her blessings for victory over evil.
Tenth and main day of Dashain, celebrating victory of good over evil, tika and jamara ceremony.
Eleventh day of Dashain, observing fast and prayers.
Twelfth day of Dashain, concluding the festival with rituals and offerings.
Worshiping wealth and prosperity, buying gold or utensils is considered auspicious.
First day of Tihar, worshiping crows for their role in the ecosystem.
Second day of Tihar, worshiping dogs for their loyalty and companionship.
Third day of Tihar, worshiping Goddess Laxmi for wealth and prosperity.
Fourth day of Tihar, worshiping oxen or self depending on community.
Fifth and final day of Tihar, sisters bless brothers for long life and prosperity.
Sun worship festival celebrated by the Mithila community.

festival: Yomari Punhi / Dhanya Purnima / Udhauli parbha
description: A Newari festival celebrating the harvest season, where people prepare and eat Yomari, a sweet dumpling made of rice flour filled with molasses and sesame seeds.
Winter solstice festival marking sun's northward journey.
Martyrs' Day commemorating those who sacrificed their lives for the nation.
Tibetan New Year celebrated by the Tamang community.
Festival dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, the goddess of knowledge, music, and arts.
Festival dedicated to Lord Shiva, celebrated with night-long vigils and prayers.
Tibetan New Year celebrated by the Tamang community.
Festival of colors celebrated in the Himalayan region.
Festival of colors celebrated in the Terai region.
Minor Dashain celebration marking the end of the year.
